description:
You are a Tier-1 support engineer who has just received a request to
    collect basic diagnostics from a customer’s Linux system so that the
    escalation team can reproduce a subtle crash.   
    Create a single plain-text report file at

        /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log

    The directory /home/user/diagnostics does not exist yet, so create it with
    default user permissions.

    The report MUST contain **exactly five** clearly delimited sections in the
    order shown below.  Each section header is a single line consisting of the
    three equals signs, a single space, the section name written exactly as
    below, another space, and three more equals signs.  Put **no extra white
    space** before or after the header line.

        === Timestamp ===
        === Hostname ===
        === Kernel ===
        === Disk ===
        === Memory ===

    Immediately after each header, append the command’s raw console output as
    described:

    1. Timestamp  
       • Use:  date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"  
       • The output must be a single line in UTC (RFC-3339 basic form).

    2. Hostname  
       • Use:  hostname  
       • One line only.

    3. Kernel  
       • Use:  uname -r  
       • One line only (kernel release string).

    4. Disk  
       • Use:  df -h /  
       • Include the normal two-line output (header + single filesystem line).

    5. Memory  
       • Use:  free -m  
       • Capture exactly the first two lines produced by the command
         (header line beginning with “total” and the line beginning with “Mem:”).

    The automated grader will verify that:

    • The file exists at the exact path above.  
    • Each of the five headers appears once, in the correct order, spelled and
      formatted exactly as shown.  
    • No header lines are blank or duplicated.  
    • Each section contains at least one non-empty line of command output.  
    • The Memory section contains a line that starts with “Mem:”.  

    Collecting this information should take only three to four shell commands
    in total.  Do **not** display personal information in the answer—only store
    it inside /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log.
truth:
<!--
        The grader will dynamically evaluate the content because values such as
        kernel release, hostname, free memory, etc. change from system to
        system.  Static strings that must be found exactly are documented
        below.  The grader will check for these fixed anchors and basic
        structure.

        Path that must exist:
            /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log

        Headers that must appear, each on its own line and in this exact order:
            === Timestamp ===
            === Hostname ===
            === Kernel ===
            === Disk ===
            === Memory ===

        Additional required traits:
            • At least one non-empty line follows each header.
            • A line beginning with “Mem:” exists somewhere between the
              “=== Memory ===” header and either EOF or the next header
              (which doesn’t exist because Memory is the final section).
    -->
name: task_000000_0d4bef35
